name: Asparagus Frittata
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Thin asparagus, unpeeled, ends trimmed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut asparagus into 1 1/2" to 2" lengths and reserve the tips.
2. Melt butter over medium heat in a non-stick, 9" saute pan.
3. Add asparagus stems (not tips) and cook, stirring occasionally, until they begin to soften, about 5 minutes.
4. In a mixing bowl, lightly beat eggs with water and 2 tablespoons of cheese. The eggs should lighten slightly but should not foam.
5. Add the reserved asparagus tips and stir well.
6. Pour the egg mixture into the saute pan. Cook for 1 minute.
7. Begin forming the frittata by gently pushing the sides of the egg mixture into the center, allowing any liquid eggs to come into contact with the pan. Continue gently pushing without stirring until the eggs on the bottom are solid and just beginning to brown, about 3-4 minutes.
8. Cover with a lid and cook for the last minute or two to set the top of the eggs.
9. Place a dinner plate directly over the saute pan and firmly hold the plate and pan together. Invert so that the eggs come out onto the plate. Remove the pan from the plate and return it to the heat. Slide the eggs back into the pan so they are now upside-down. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, depending on how runny or firm you want the frittata to be.
10. Alternatively, place the pan (oven-proof only) under the broiler and cook just long enough to puff the top and brown lightly.
11. Slide the frittata onto a clean plate. Dust with the remaining 2 tablespoons of cheese. Season lightly with salt.
